title: narcissus major var. propinquus
intro: Narcissus major var. propinquus, also known as the garden narcissus or daffodil, is a perennial plant belonging to the Amaryllidaceae family, characterized by its showy, trumpet-shaped flowers in shades of yellow and white. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs due to its expectorant and antispasmodic properties. The health benefits of Narcissus major var. propinquus include reducing inflammation, relieving congestion, and acting as a natural antiseptic, making it a popular herb in herbal medicine. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, and are often combined with other herbs to enhance their medicinal properties.
